summary:
• Escalating U.S. sanctions on Chinese refiners handling Iranian crude and the EU's Russian LNG import ban are fragmenting global energy trade routes and forcing buyers to navigate increasingly complex geopolitical constraints on sourcing.

• Canada's approval of the Enbridge Westcoast expansion and Nigeria's emergence as a net fuel exporter via Dangote refinery represent supply diversification that could ease Western Hemisphere tightness, but geopolitical risks to Middle Eastern flows remain elevated amid U.S.-Iran tensions.

• Structural demand destruction in global LNG markets, combined with renewable energy transitions advancing faster than oil demand decline, creates a bifurcated risk environment where traditional crude supply constraints may ease while refining margins face longer-term compression.
